description_long: This is a portable radio receiver with a boxy, retro design, featuring a prominent metallic speaker grille on the front and a large tuning knob to the right. The top section includes a frequency indicator with red markings and two protruding dials or switches, as well as a telescopic metal antenna and two colored wires (red and blue) for possible external connections. The sides are equipped with robust, pivoting handles in a tan color, likely for carrying or adjusting the angle of the device. The back is plain and solid, with the handle forming a frame around the rear surface. The overall construction appears to be a combination of metal and plastic, with a dark blue or black body and tan accents, giving it a durable and functional appearance suitable for outdoor or urban use. The object is compact, suggesting it is designed for portability and ease of use in various environments.
description: A compact, retro-style portable radio receiver with a metallic speaker grille, large tuning knob, telescopic antenna, and tan carrying handles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the metallic speaker grille, large tuning knob, frequency indicator, and antenna with colored wires.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the pivoting tan handle and the thickness of the radio body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back surface and the handle forming a frame around the radio.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handle and the protruding antenna and wires on top.
